title: centaurea adscendens
intro: Centaurea adscendens, also known as mountain cornflower, is a perennial plant species in the Asteraceae family, characterized by its blue-violet flowers and tall, slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Centaurea adscendens include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Centaurea adscendens can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with the dried flowers and leaves typically used in these preparations.
